description_long: This is a rustic, octagonal wooden mug or small bucket with a dark, weathered wood texture and reinforced by two thick, black metal bands encircling its body. The vessel features a prominent, angular black metal handle attached to one side, providing a sturdy grip. The wood grain is visible and detailed, with knots and natural imperfections adding to its aged appearance. On one side, there is a green fleur-de-lis emblem painted or carved onto a wooden panel, giving it a decorative touch. The interior is also wooden, matching the exterior, and the open top suggests it is designed to hold liquids or small items. The overall construction and materials evoke a medieval or fantasy tavern aesthetic, suitable for use as a mug, tankard, or small bucket in an urban or themed environment.
description: A dark, octagonal wooden mug or small bucket with black metal bands and a sturdy angular handle, featuring a green fleur-de-lis emblem on one side.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open top, wood grain, and the angular black handle clearly attached to the right side.
description_view_1: The handle is centered and fully visible from the back, with the wood panels and metal bands wrapping around the body.
description_view_2: The handle is on the left, and the wood grain and metal bands are prominent, with no emblem visible.
description_view_3: The green fleur-de-lis emblem is centered, and the handle is not visible from this angle.
